statement.markdown: A bitonic sequence is a sequence that has an increasing sequence followed by a decreasing sequence. A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from another sequence by deleting some elements without changing the order of the remaining elements. Given an array `arr` of size `n`, find the length of the longest bitonic subsequence.

hints.text: To solve this problem, you need to find a way to track the longest increasing subsequence and the longest decreasing subsequence for each element in the array.
Consider using dynamic programming to store the lengths of the longest increasing subsequences and longest decreasing subsequences ending at each position in the array.
The longest bitonic subsequence ending at a particular index is the sum of the longest increasing subsequence ending at that index and the longest decreasing subsequence starting from that index.

editorial.title: Longest Bitonic Subsequence
editorial.intuition: The problem requires finding the longest subsequence that first increases and then decreases. A brute force approach would be inefficient, but we can use dynamic programming to solve it optimally.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve generating all possible subsequences and checking if they are bitonic. This approach is correct but has an exponential time complexity, making it impractical for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use dynamic programming to solve this problem. The idea is to maintain two arrays: one for the longest increasing subsequence ending at each position and another for the longest decreasing subsequence starting at each position.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves computing the longest increasing subsequence for the prefix of the array and the longest decreasing subsequence for the suffix of the array. We then combine these two to find the longest bitonic subsequence.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proved by observing that a bitonic subsequence can be divided into an increasing part and a decreasing part. The longest increasing subsequence ending at each position and the longest decreasing subsequence starting at each position can be computed separately and then combined to find the longest bitonic subsequence.
editorial.dryRun: The given Python code implements the optimal solution. It first reads the input array and then computes the longest increasing subsequence for the prefix of the array and the longest decreasing subsequence for the suffix of the array. It then combines these two to find the longest bitonic subsequence.
commonMistakes: Not considering the decreasing part of the bitonic subsequence
Not using dynamic programming to optimize the solution
Incorrectly combining the increasing and decreasing subsequences
Not handling the edge cases correctly
Not using the correct data structures to store the subsequences
Not optimizing the solution for large inputs
